title: Reliable Bash Automation and Tests · Spiral Review
summary: Turn a fragile command history into a bounded script that validates inputs, preserves data, reports failures, and can be rerun safely. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a tested idempotent Bash operations script after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.
Use set options, traps, explicit conditionals, and status handling while understanding where errexit and pipefail do not behave intuitively.
Handle positional parameters, defaults, validation, arrays, and quoted expansion without word splitting or glob injection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — A report script receives filenames "north 3.csv" and "--dry-run" plus one missing file. Preserve argument boundaries, validate before mutation, clean temporary state, and prove rerunning is idempotent. Competency probe: Test scripts with fixtures, changed inputs, failure injection, shell analysis, exit status, and idempotence checks.

Inspect the Bash Test Evidence trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test scripts with fixtures, changed inputs, failure injection, shell analysis, exit status, and idempotence checks.

content.text: For Bash Test Evidence, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A syntax check exercises command behavior and cleanup.
